scenario_results.reason: The package assembled a community-care report with metric definitions, source tie-outs, and caveats tied to encounters, care plans, authorizations, and incidents.
Ambiguous engagement logic was not resolved silently; the lane held publication and requested metric-owner determination.
Stale claims, discharge, and incident sources were surfaced explicitly and converted into a low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting EHR, registry, and claims records were preserved as a reconciliation problem instead of compressed into false certainty.
The missing program authority, reporting period, and submission path blocked the package until prerequisites were supplied.
Unsupported causal and compliance claims were refused and replaced with evidence-bounded language.
Community-care terminology such as HCBS CAHPS, CCBHC, QMR, person-centered plans, and survey artifacts remained intact in the output.
Missing program authority, incident posture, and privacy or publication ownership correctly triggered a low-confidence escalation.
Clinical interpretation and incident substantiation were routed to adjacent specialists with reporting-safe context preserved.
Conflicting CMS, state, and tenant rules were recorded and escalated rather than harmonized without authority.
Tenant-specific stricter rules were applied without losing the public baseline or obscuring precedence assumptions.
The lane refused a generic dashboard shortcut and required the community-care records that materially define the industry.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed the validated standard against the defined workflow and boundary contract.
The package remains validated on paper with no retained-implementation runtime claim and explicit escalation and orchestrator-return conditions.
